steps.title: Read the two endpoints from the graph
steps.content: Average rate of change only needs the two endpoint values. On the graph, the point at $x=2$ is $(2,6)$, so the momentum is $6$ newton-seconds. The point at $x=6$ is $(6,8)$, so the momentum is $8$ newton-seconds.

steps.title: Compute average rate of change
steps.content: Use change in momentum divided by change in time: $$\frac{y(6)-y(2)}{6-2}=\frac{8-6}{6-2}=\frac{2}{4}=0.5.$$ The units are newton-seconds per second, and the answer is 0.5.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The graph values are $(2,6)$ and $(6,8)$, so the momentum increases by $2$ over $4$ seconds. Equivalent correct entries include $0.5$, $.5$, and $1/2$.
